---
title: "Research Journey"
description: "Academic training, research highlights, and scientific contributions across institutions"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<style>
/* ── Timeline ── */
.timeline { position: relative; padding: 0; list-style: none; }
.timeline::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  left: 28px;
  top: 0; bottom: 0;
  width: 2px;
  background: linear-gradient(to bottom, #1a5276, #148f77, #8e44ad, #d35400);
}
.tl-item { display: flex; gap: 24px; margin-bottom: 3rem; position: relative; }
.tl-dot {
  width: 56px; height: 56px; border-radius: 50%;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  flex-shrink: 0; font-size: 1.4rem;
  border: 3px solid white;
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px currentColor;
  z-index: 1;
}
.tl-dot.work    { background: #eaf3fb; color: #1a5276; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #1a5276; }
.tl-dot.post    { background: #e1f5ee; color: #148f77; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #148f77; }
.tl-dot.phd     { background: #eeedfe; color: #534ab7; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #534ab7; }
.tl-dot.mtech   { background: #faeeda; color: #854f0b; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #854f0b; }
.tl-dot.be      { background: #faece7; color: #993c1d; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #993c1d; }

.tl-body { flex: 1; }
.tl-period { font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.07em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6c757d; margin-bottom: 4px; }
.tl-title  { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1a2b3c; margin: 0 0 2px; font-family: 'DM Serif Display', serif; }
.tl-inst   { font-size: 0.95rem; color: #148f77; font-weight: 500; margin-bottom: 10px; }
.tl-grade  { display: inline-block; background: #eaf3fb; color: #1a5276; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 12px; margin-bottom: 12px; }

/* ── Institution cards ── */
.inst-card {
  border: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
  border-radius: 14px;
  overflow: hidden;
  margin-bottom: 2.5rem;
  background: white;
}
.inst-card img {
  width: 100%; height: 220px;
  object-fit: cover;
  display: block;
}
.inst-card-body { padding: 1.4rem 1.6rem; }
.inst-card-body h3 { margin-top: 0; color: #1a5276; }

/* ── Research highlight cards ── */
.research-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr));
  gap: 1.25rem;
  margin: 1.5rem 0 2.5rem;
}
.rcard {
  border: 1px solid #e2e8f0;
  border-radius: 12px;
  overflow: hidden;
  background: white;
  transition: box-shadow 0.2s, transform 0.2s;
}
.rcard:hover { box-shadow: 0 6px 24px rgba(26,82,118,0.12); transform: translateY(-2px); }
.rcard img { width: 100%; height: 160px; object-fit: cover; display: block; }
.rcard-body { padding: 1rem 1.1rem; }
.rcard-body h4 { margin: 0 0 6px; color: #1a5276; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 600; }
.rcard-body p  { margin: 0; font-size: 0.85rem; color: #495057; line-height: 1.6; }
.rcard-tag {
  display: inline-block;
  background: #e1f5ee; color: #085041;
  font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 600;
  padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 8px;
  margin: 8px 4px 0 0;
}

/* ── Skills bars ── */
.skill-bar-wrap { margin-bottom: 10px; }
.skill-label { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font-size: 0.85rem; margin-bottom: 4px; color: #2c3e50; }
.skill-bar { height: 8px; background: #e9ecef; border-radius: 4px; overflow: hidden; }
.skill-fill { height: 100%; border-radius: 4px; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #1a5276, #148f77); }

/* ── Award badges ── */
.award-list { list-style: none; padding: 0; }
.award-list li {
  display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 12px;
  padding: 10px 0; border-bottom: 0.5px solid #e9ecef;
  font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.5;
}
.award-icon { font-size: 1.3rem; flex-shrink: 0; }

/* ── Section divider ── */
.section-rule { border: none; border-top: 2px solid #d6eaf8; margin: 2.5rem 0; }
</style>
```

## Career & education timeline

```{=html}
<ul class="timeline">

  <li class="tl-item">
    <div class="tl-dot work">🔬</div>
    <div class="tl-body">
      <div class="tl-period">Feb 2022 – Mar 2025</div>
      <div class="tl-title">Computational Research Scientist II</div>
      <div class="tl-inst">BHSAI · Henry M. Jackson Foundation for the Advancement of Military Medicine · Frederick, MD</div>
      <ul>
        <li><strong>Data curation &amp; visualization:</strong> Managed and visualized large-scale biological and chemical datasets — feature importance, data cleaning, PCA, t-SNE, UMAP, clustering, heatmaps, pattern recognition, and statistical interpretation.</li>
        <li><strong>Generative AI in drug discovery:</strong> Utilized generative AI techniques to generate novel molecular structures, enhancing model predictions and supporting drug repurposing applications.</li>
        <li><strong>ML models for compound classification:</strong> Developed traditional ML, DNN, GNN, and transformer-based LLM classifiers to identify active/inactive compounds for neurotoxicity-relevant GPCR targets using public databases.</li>
        <li><strong>Computational neurotoxicity assessment:</strong> Neurotoxicity assessment of 200+ chemicals using high-dimensional Multi-Electrode Array (MEA) assay data (electrical activity of neurons).</li>
        <li><strong>Integrative computational neurotoxicity profiling:</strong> Integrated high-throughput data with dose-response modeling (tcplfit2) to characterize neurotoxic effects; explored chemical structure–MEA response profile relationships; incorporated chemical–protein interactions and molecular features for integrative computational biology analyses.</li>
        <li><strong>Toxicogenomics studies:</strong> Developed and implemented RNA-Seq pipelines for in vivo and in vitro animal (rat, guinea pig) and human (pooled hepatocyte) data. Conducted DEG analysis for liver and kidney injury biomarkers. Performed in vitro–in vivo correlation and cross-species analysis to enhance translational relevance.</li>
        <li><strong>Integrating genomics, bioinformatics, and toxicology</strong> to enhance understanding of chemical impact on human health and improve safety assessments.</li>
        <li><strong>ADMET profiling</strong> with vNN-ADMET/SwissADME for risk assessment of chemical compounds.</li>
        <li>Supported web development and program upgrades in collaboration with the IT team.</li>
        <li>Collaborated with cross-functional teams — biologists, toxicologists, and software engineers — on DoD, DTRA, NIH, and AFRL funded projects.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </li>

  <li class="tl-item">
    <div class="tl-dot post">⚗️</div>
    <div class="tl-body">
      <div class="tl-period">May 2018 – Feb 2022</div>
      <div class="tl-title">Postdoctoral Researcher</div>
      <div class="tl-inst">Computer-Aided Drug Design Center · University of Maryland, Baltimore · Advisor: Prof. Alexander D. MacKerell Jr.</div>
      <ul>
        <li><strong>Computational drug discovery framework:</strong> Spearheaded development and deployment of computational workflows for early-phase drug discovery — protein structure preparation, SILCS FragMaps, hotspot analysis, pharmacophore modeling, virtual screening, molecular docking, and SAR analysis for hit identification and lead prioritization.</li>
        <li><strong>Protein-ligand binding affinity:</strong> Employed SILCS technology to compute protein-ligand relative binding affinities and benchmarked against current FEP methods to validate accuracy for drug design applications.</li>
        <li><strong>Polarizable force field and MD simulations:</strong> Designed SILCS workflow for polarizable force fields and MD to assess protein-ligand energetics, molecular interactions, and docking scores.</li>
        <li><strong>Water network modeling:</strong> Developed and validated <strong>SILCS-MC Water</strong> — a novel technique to accurately predict water networks in binding pockets and their impact on ligand binding affinity and pose.</li>
        <li><strong>Imaging probe development:</strong> Investigated alpha-synuclein fibril binding using polarizable SILCS calculations and SAR analysis to elucidate probe–target interactions.</li>
        <li><strong>COVID-19 drug discovery:</strong> Applied SILCS-based CADD workflow targeting SARS-CoV-2 spike protein for antiviral drug development as part of the drug discovery team.</li>
        <li><strong>Target-specific modeling:</strong> Designed predictive models for hERG1 blockade integrating SILCS modeling and physicochemical properties to support safety pharmacology profiling.</li>
        <li>Partnered with multidisciplinary teams to facilitate data-driven decision making and translate in silico predictions to actionable insights.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </li>

  <li class="tl-item">
    <div class="tl-dot phd">🎓</div>
    <div class="tl-body">
      <div class="tl-period">Aug 2013 – May 2018</div>
      <div class="tl-title">PhD · Chemical Engineering</div>
      <div class="tl-inst">Mississippi State University · Starkville, MS · Advisor: Prof. Neeraj Rai</div>
      <span class="tl-grade">GPA 3.66 / 4.0</span>
      <p><em>Thesis: "Modeling Phase and Sorption Equilibria using First Principles Simulations"</em></p>
      <ul>
        <li><strong>VLE &amp; thermodynamic properties:</strong> Predicted vapor–liquid equilibria (VLE), saturated vapor pressures, critical properties, boiling points, and structural properties via first-principles DFT/classical Monte Carlo simulations.</li>
        <li><strong>Adsorption &amp; transport in ionic liquids:</strong> Predicted adsorption equilibria, transport properties, vibrational spectra, and structural mechanisms for ionic liquids with gases via first-principles simulations.</li>
        <li><strong>DFT exchange-correlation functionals:</strong> Employed multiple DFT functionals for computing condensed-phase properties and dimer energetics with high accuracy.</li>
        <li><strong>Algorithm development in CP2K:</strong> Developed novel "cluster translation moves" algorithm useful for sampling strongly associating fluids in the CP2K Monte Carlo suite.</li>
        <li><strong>Force field development:</strong> Classical TraPPE force field development for organic molecules.</li>
        <li><strong>Partition coefficient &amp; free energy prediction:</strong> Predicted octanol-water partition coefficients and hydration/solvation free energies for NDMA and trichloroethylene — important environmental contaminants.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </li>

  <li class="tl-item">
    <div class="tl-dot mtech">🏛️</div>
    <div class="tl-body">
      <div class="tl-period">Dec 2011 – Jul 2013</div>
      <div class="tl-title">M.Tech · Chemical Engineering</div>
      <div class="tl-inst">Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad (IITH) · India</div>
      <span class="tl-grade">CGPA 9.17 / 10.0</span>
      <ul>
        <li><strong>Interfacial science:</strong> Computed interfacial properties for multiple liquid systems via dissipative particle dynamics simulations.</li>
        <li><strong>Teaching Assistant:</strong> Advanced Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics, IITH (Aug 2012 – Dec 2012).</li>
        <li><strong>Laboratory Instructor:</strong> Introduction to Chemical Engineering Lab, IITH (Jan 2013 – May 2013).</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </li>

  <li class="tl-item">
    <div class="tl-dot be">🎖️</div>
    <div class="tl-body">
      <div class="tl-period">Jun 2007 – Jun 2010</div>
      <div class="tl-title">B.E. · Chemical Engineering</div>
      <div class="tl-inst">Bharati Vidyapeeth College of Engineering (BVCOE) · Pune, India</div>
      <ul>
        <li>1st Prize, Industry-Defined Problem "Economical Desulfurization in Refineries" (HPCL sponsored), MIT College of Engineering, Pune, 2009.</li>
        <li>1st Prize, Paper Presentation "CO₂ Sequestration by Producing Carbon-Based Fertilizer Using Biomass", Bhartiyam 2010, Pune.</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </li>

</ul>
```
<hr class="section-rule">
## Research highlights

### Computational toxicology & AI/ML (HJF · BHSAI · 2022–2025)

```{=html}
<div class="research-grid">
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Toxicogenomics & RNA-Seq pipelines</h4>
      <p>Developed RNA-Seq pipelines for in vivo (rat, guinea pig) and in vitro (human hepatocyte) data. Conducted DEG analysis for liver and kidney injury biomarkers. Performed IVIVE and cross-species analysis. Published in <em>Int. J. Mol. Sci.</em> 2023, 2024.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">RNA-Seq</span><span class="rcard-tag">Kallisto</span><span class="rcard-tag">DEG Analysis</span><span class="rcard-tag">IVIVE</span><span class="rcard-tag">Cross-Species</span><span class="rcard-tag">Liver Toxicity</span><span class="rcard-tag">Kidney Injury</span><span class="rcard-tag">Biomarkers</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>MEA neurotoxicity & dose-response modeling</h4>
      <p>Neurotoxicity assessment of 200+ chemicals using high-dimensional Multi-Electrode Array (MEA) assay data. Integrated dose-response modeling (tcplfit2) and explored chemical structure–MEA response profile similarity relationships.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">MEA</span><span class="rcard-tag">Neurotoxicity</span><span class="rcard-tag">tcplfit2</span><span class="rcard-tag">Dose-Response</span><span
      class="rcard-tag">Cheminformatics</span><span  class="rcard-tag">High-Throughput Screening</span><span class="rcard-tag">Electrophysiology</span><span class="rcard-tag">NAM</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>ML/DL models for GPCR compound classification</h4>
      <p>Developed traditional ML, DNN, GNN, and transformer-based LLM classifiers to identify active/inactive compounds against neurotoxicity-relevant GPCR targets, enhancing drug–target interaction predictions.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">DNN</span><span class="rcard-tag">GNN</span><span class="rcard-tag">Transformer LLM</span><span class="rcard-tag">GPCR</span><span class="rcard-tag">DeepChem</span><span class="rcard-tag">QSAR</span><span class="rcard-tag">Drug–Target Interaction</span><span class="rcard-tag">PyTorch</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Generative AI for molecular design & data augmentation</h4>
      <p>Leveraged <strong>REINVENT4</strong> and complementary generative frameworks to design synthesizable, chemically valid molecules (valid SMILES). Generated synthetic molecular libraries to augment training data for ML classifiers, directly boosting model accuracy and chemical diversity across neurotoxicity-relevant target classes.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">REINVENT4</span><span class="rcard-tag">Generative AI</span><span class="rcard-tag">De Novo Design</span><span class="rcard-tag">Data Augmentation</span><span class="rcard-tag">Valid SMILES</span><span class="rcard-tag">Drug Repurposing</span><span class="rcard-tag">ML Accuracy</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Data curation & chemical space visualization</h4>
      <p>Managed and analyzed large-scale biological and chemical datasets using PCA, t-SNE, UMAP, clustering, and heatmaps. Performed feature importance, pattern recognition, and statistical interpretation across multi-OMICS data.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">PCA</span><span class="rcard-tag">UMAP</span><span class="rcard-tag">t-SNE</span><span class="rcard-tag">Clustering</span><span class="rcard-tag">Multi-OMICS</span><span class="rcard-tag">Feature Importance</span><span class="rcard-tag">Cheminformatics</span><span class="rcard-tag">Data Visualization</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Integrative safety science</h4>
      <p>Connected genomics, bioinformatics, and toxicology to deepen understanding of how chemicals impact human health. Integrated multi-OMICS signals, in vitro assay data, and computational models to strengthen mechanistic safety assessments and support NAM-based risk evaluation.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">Genomics</span><span class="rcard-tag">Bioinformatics</span><span class="rcard-tag">Toxicology</span><span class="rcard-tag">Multi-OMICS</span><span class="rcard-tag">Safety Assessment</span><span class="rcard-tag">NAM</span><span class="rcard-tag">IVIVE</span><span class="rcard-tag">Biomarker Discovery</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Drug design & CADD (UMB · 2018–2022)

```{=html}
<div class="research-grid">
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>SILCS-based drug discovery framework</h4>
      <p>Spearheaded end-to-end computational workflows — protein structure preparation, SILCS FragMaps, hotspot analysis, pharmacophore modeling, virtual screening, molecular docking, and SAR analysis — for hit identification and lead prioritization.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">SILCS</span><span class="rcard-tag">FragMaps</span><span class="rcard-tag">Virtual Screening</span><span class="rcard-tag">Docking</span><span class="rcard-tag">SAR</span><span class="rcard-tag">Pharmacophore</span><span class="rcard-tag">Hit Identification</span><span class="rcard-tag">Lead Optimization</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Protein–ligand binding affinity & FEP benchmarking</h4>
      <p>Employed SILCS to compute protein-ligand relative binding affinities and benchmarked against FEP methods to validate accuracy for drug design. Published in <em>Chem. Sci.</em> 2021.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">SILCS</span><span class="rcard-tag">FEP</span><span class="rcard-tag">Binding Affinity</span><span class="rcard-tag">Free Energy</span><span class="rcard-tag">Lead Optimization</span><span class="rcard-tag">Protein–Ligand</span><span class="rcard-tag">MD</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>SILCS-MC Water — novel water network modeling</h4>
      <p>Developed and validated a novel technique to accurately predict water networks in protein binding pockets and quantify their impact on ligand binding affinity and pose.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">Water Networks</span><span class="rcard-tag">Monte Carlo</span><span class="rcard-tag">SILCS</span><span class="rcard-tag">Solvation</span><span class="rcard-tag">Binding Pose</span><span class="rcard-tag">Method Development</span><span class="rcard-tag">Drug Design</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>hERG cardiotoxicity prediction</h4>
      <p>Designed predictive models for hERG1 blockade integrating SILCS modeling and physicochemical properties to support safety pharmacology profiling. Published in <em>Chemistry</em> 2022.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">hERG</span><span class="rcard-tag">Cardiotoxicity</span><span class="rcard-tag">Safety Pharmacology</span><span class="rcard-tag">ML</span><span class="rcard-tag">Ion Channel</span><span class="rcard-tag">QSAR</span><span class="rcard-tag">Drug Safety</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Polarizable force field & MD simulations</h4>
      <p>Designed SILCS workflow for Drude polarizable force fields and MD to assess protein-ligand energetics, molecular interactions, and docking scores with higher physical accuracy than additive force fields.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">Polarizable FF</span><span class="rcard-tag">Drude</span><span class="rcard-tag">MD</span><span class="rcard-tag">CHARMM</span><span class="rcard-tag">Protein Energetics</span><span class="rcard-tag">Force Field</span><span class="rcard-tag">Electronic Polarizability</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>COVID-19 antiviral & imaging probe development</h4>
      <p>Applied SILCS CADD workflow to SARS-CoV-2 spike protein for antiviral drug development. Investigated alpha-synuclein fibril binding using polarizable SILCS and SAR analysis for imaging probe development.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">COVID-19</span><span class="rcard-tag">SARS-CoV-2</span><span class="rcard-tag">Antiviral</span><span class="rcard-tag">Alpha-Synuclein</span><span class="rcard-tag">Parkinson's</span><span class="rcard-tag">CADD</span><span class="rcard-tag">SAR</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### First-principles simulations (MSU PhD · 2013–2018)

```{=html}
<div class="research-grid">
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Vapor–liquid equilibria via DFT & Monte Carlo</h4>
      <p>Predicted VLE, saturated vapor pressures, critical properties, and boiling points for hydrofluorocarbons, CO₂, SO₂, and argon using first-principles DFT/Monte Carlo in CP2K. 7 publications.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">DFT</span><span class="rcard-tag">Monte Carlo</span><span class="rcard-tag">CP2K</span><span class="rcard-tag">VLE</span><span class="rcard-tag">HFCs</span><span class="rcard-tag">Refrigerants</span><span class="rcard-tag">Thermodynamics</span><span class="rcard-tag">Exchange-Correlation</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>CO₂ absorption in ionic liquids</h4>
      <p>Predicted adsorption equilibria, transport properties, vibrational spectra, and structural mechanisms for CO₂ in phosphonium-based ionic liquids via first-principles simulations.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">Ionic Liquids</span><span class="rcard-tag">CO₂ Capture</span><span class="rcard-tag">DFT</span><span class="rcard-tag">Adsorption</span><span class="rcard-tag">Carbon Capture</span><span class="rcard-tag">Transport Properties</span><span class="rcard-tag">Green Chemistry</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Algorithm development: cluster translation moves</h4>
      <p>Developed a novel "cluster translation moves" algorithm in the CP2K Monte Carlo suite, enabling efficient sampling of strongly associating fluids that were previously computationally intractable.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">Algorithm Development</span><span class="rcard-tag">CP2K</span><span class="rcard-tag">Monte Carlo</span><span class="rcard-tag">Sampling</span><span class="rcard-tag">Associating Fluids</span><span class="rcard-tag">Method Development</span>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Force field development & environmental contaminants</h4>
      <p>Developed classical TraPPE force fields for chloroethenes and fluorinated alkanes. Predicted octanol-water partition coefficients and solvation free energies for NDMA and trichloroethylene.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">TraPPE</span><span class="rcard-tag">Force Field</span><span class="rcard-tag">NDMA</span><span class="rcard-tag">Trichloroethylene</span><span class="rcard-tag">Partition Coefficient</span><span class="rcard-tag">Solvation Free Energy</span><span class="rcard-tag">Environmental Contaminants</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Interfacial science (IITH M.Tech · 2011–2013)

```{=html}
<div class="research-grid">
  <div class="rcard">
    <div class="rcard-body">
      <h4>Interfacial properties via dissipative particle dynamics</h4>
      <p>Computed interfacial tension and related properties for multiple immiscible and partially miscible liquid systems using dissipative particle dynamics (DPD) simulations. Published in <em>Chem. Phys. Lett.</em> 2014.</p>
      <span class="rcard-tag">DPD</span><span class="rcard-tag">Interfacial Tension</span><span class="rcard-tag">Mesoscale Simulation</span><span class="rcard-tag">Liquid Mixtures</span><span class="rcard-tag">Emulsions</span><span class="rcard-tag">Thermodynamics</span><span class="rcard-tag">Process Design</span>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

<hr class="section-rule">

## Awards & honors

```{=html}
<ul class="award-list">
  <li>
    <span class="award-icon">🥈</span>
    <span><strong>Silver Medal — 2nd Position</strong>, Diploma in Chemical Engineering, New Delhi, India, 2008</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="award-icon">🥇</span>
    <span><strong>1st Rank</strong>, Entrance Examination for Diploma Holders in Chemical Engineering, BVCOE Pune, 2007</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="award-icon">🏆</span>
    <span><strong>1st Prize</strong>, Industry-Defined Problem "Economical Desulfurization in Refineries" sponsored by HPCL, MIT College of Engineering, Pune, 2009</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="award-icon">🏆</span>
    <span><strong>1st Prize</strong>, Paper Presentation "CO₂ Sequestration by Producing Carbon-Based Fertilizer Using Biomass", Bhartiyam 2010, Pune</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="award-icon">🪑</span>
    <span><strong>Poster Chair</strong>, Computational Toxicology I session, Society of Toxicology Annual Meeting, 2024</span>
  </li>
</ul>
```
